markdown.headingText: What is happening in this file:
markdown.markdown:
HTML Output:

---------------- - **HTML Input/Button**: These are "dumb" elements. They do nothing until the `htmlBtn.onclick` function in the OJS block manually pulls the value from the input and pushes it into the span. - **OJS Input (`Inputs.text`)**: This is "smart." Because of the `viewof` keyword, the variable `ojs_text` is globally available to the OJS runtime. - **OJS Button (`Inputs.button`)**: Unlike the HTML button, this one returns a value (an incrementing integer). By simply mentioning `ojs_btn` in a code block, you tell OJS to re-run that specific block every time the button is pressed.

HTML Output:

```{ojs} // Logic for the HTML Elements above // We grab the elements by ID and attach a manual event listener htmlBtn = document.getElementById('h-btn'); htmlInput = document.getElementById('h-in'); htmlOutput = document.getElementById('h-out'); ``` ```{ojs} htmlBtn.onclick = () => { htmlOutput.innerText = htmlInput.value; }; // --- OJS Native Components --- // 'viewof' creates a reactive variable that other cells can "see" viewof ojs_text = Inputs.text({ label: "OJS Input" }) // This button yields a value (the number of clicks) viewof ojs_btn = Inputs.button("OJS Button") // --- OJS Reactive Output --- // This markdown block updates automatically whenever ojs_text changes md`OJS Text Value: ${ojs_text}` // This block updates only when the OJS button is clicked { ojs_btn; // Referencing the button makes this block depend on it return md`OJS Button was clicked!`; } ```
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
HTML Output:

---------------- ## What is happening in this file: - **HTML Input/Button**: These are "dumb" elements. They do nothing until the `htmlBtn.onclick` function in the OJS block manually pulls the value from the input and pushes it into the span. - **OJS Input (`Inputs.text`)**: This is "smart." Because of the `viewof` keyword, the variable `ojs_text` is globally available to the OJS runtime. - **OJS Button (`Inputs.button`)**: Unlike the HTML button, this one returns a value (an incrementing integer). By simply mentioning `ojs_btn` in a code block, you tell OJS to re-run that specific block every time the button is pressed.

HTML Output:

```{ojs} // Logic for the HTML Elements above // We grab the elements by ID and attach a manual event listener htmlBtn = document.getElementById('h-btn'); htmlInput = document.getElementById('h-in'); htmlOutput = document.getElementById('h-out'); ``` ```{ojs} htmlBtn.onclick = () => { htmlOutput.innerText = htmlInput.value; }; // --- OJS Native Components --- // 'viewof' creates a reactive variable that other cells can "see" viewof ojs_text = Inputs.text({ label: "OJS Input" }) // This button yields a value (the number of clicks) viewof ojs_btn = Inputs.button("OJS Button") // --- OJS Reactive Output --- // This markdown block updates automatically whenever ojs_text changes md`OJS Text Value: ${ojs_text}` // This block updates only when the OJS button is clicked { ojs_btn; // Referencing the button makes this block depend on it return md`OJS Button was clicked!`; } ```
